74130.subject: Sandboxes, Infrastructure as Code
74130.body:
It's Friday and you know what that means! Here's a summary of the stuff we shipped this week  

Railway

PRODUCT UPDATE

***************
Changelog time!
***************

It's Friday and you know what that means! Here's a summary of the stuff we shipped this week:

•Sandboxes to Priority Boarding

•Infrastructure as Code in the CLI

•Fixes and improvements

Read the changelog ( https://railway.com/changelog/2026-06-05-sandboxes?utm_campaign=Changelog&utm_content=Changelog+Template&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

If you have any thoughts/feedback on the changes we’re making, reach out on Central Station! ( https://station.railway.com?utm_campaign=Changelog&utm_content=Changelog+Template&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

- Your friends at Railway

Historically, IPO access has largely gone to institutional
investors.
Not anymore.

For the first time, you can apply to buy xStocks before they
are available to the open market: starting with SpaceX.

I’m interested  
( https://link.kraken.com/f/a/_m5JjwWkd81--XRKvoOPsg~~/AAQRxRA~/s01T7H0gicVVW0cG4jmlP8IL01mIF8713x9AFdXESZ6_hFU1vkkKdORtFJYopBCJmB0lhZjfP64EEIoHO5hjhwJB0wobRL8DysT-Y-dOZUC45JSEGl68ru5lzKTVMEW1_dtiJAR_5jermpMRy5vMHw~~ )

--------------------
Here’s how it works:
--------------------

* Submit interest for SpaceX xStocks in the Kraken app
* Funds are reserved. Anything not filled is refunded
automatically
* Cancel anytime before the window closes
* Any allocation received is live in your portfolio on listing
day
Allocation is decided by the IPO broker based on demand. High
demand may result in a full, partial, or no allocation.

74128.subject: BTC Falls to Lowest Since 2024 as Tokenization Wave Grows
74128.body:
**Happy Friday, Defiers,**

Here are the biggest news in crypto and web3 today:

* **[JPMorgan, Citi, BofA](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/four-major-us-banks-and-the-clearing-house-plan-shared-tokenized-deposit-network)**[ join Clearing House on shared tokenized-deposit network](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/four-major-us-banks-and-the-clearing-house-plan-shared-tokenized-deposit-network)

* **[Bitcoin](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/bitcoin-200-week-moving-average-break-jobs-report-2026)**[ breaks 200-week moving average for first time since 2022](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/bitcoin-200-week-moving-average-break-jobs-report-2026)

* **[apxUSD](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/apxusd-loses-dollar-peg-bitcoin-slide-strc-backed-collateral)**[ loses dollar peg as STRC-backed collateral chain breaks](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/apxusd-loses-dollar-peg-bitcoin-slide-strc-backed-collateral)

* **[CME's Duffy](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/cme-ceo-terry-duffy-calls-us-crypto-perps-disaster-waiting-to-happen)**[ calls US-regulated crypto perps 'a disaster waiting to happen'](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/cme-ceo-terry-duffy-calls-us-crypto-perps-disaster-waiting-to-happen)

JPMorgan, Citi, Bank of America, Wells Fargo and more than a dozen peer banks [announced a joint initiative](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/four-major-us-banks-and-the-clearing-house-plan-shared-tokenized-deposit-network) to put commercial-bank deposits onchain through a shared network operated by **The Clearing House**, targeting a first-half 2027 launch. The same day, **Jamie Selway**, Director of the SEC's Division of Trading and Markets, [laid out two structural workstreams](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/sec-trading-division-director-selway-tokenized-securities-framework-cftc-harmonization): a framework to list and trade tokenized securities under an 'innovation without arbitrage' principle, and a joint SEC-CFTC effort to harmonize swap reporting, portfolio margining, and product definitions across the two agencies.

The crypto side of the chart looks different. [Bitcoin](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/bitcoin-200-week-moving-average-break-jobs-report-2026) broke its 200-week moving average for the first time since the 2022 bear market this morning, falling below $61,000 after a blowout May payrolls report repriced Fed-rate-cut expectations and exposed a $1.2 billion options wall at $60,000. ETH closed the trading session down 9.5%. This week’s BTC selloff is already creating cracks in the broader ecosystem. One example: [Apyx Finance's apxUSD](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/apxusd-loses-dollar-peg-bitcoin-slide-strc-backed-collateral) slipped to $0.94, the first peg break in an on-chain dollar directly collateralized by STRC-style preferred-stock paper. Two days after BitMine filed the same structure on ETH, the BTC-side version started failing. And from inside the incumbent perimete

Good evening, Cristiano Ronaldo has earned more during his playing career than any other athlete in history, and he shows no signs of slowing down. The 41-year-old footballer has become one of just four active athletes on the Forbes list of the world’s billionaires. Here’s how he built his fortune. Lionel Messi is another one of the four active athletes on our billionaires list. Here’s how he did it. Thanks for reading! 74126.subject: 🟪 Friday charts
74126.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/666ae944-5cae-4c6c-abd6-970020388daf/BD-Banner-Sponsor_Template.png?t=1780094904)
Follow image link: (https://peaq.xyz)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1665c1fd-0a68-4290-a2ab-fc58e278431a/444.png?t=1780688977)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6c6a2e24-19fe-45aa-9219-0f11b18ed7e4/unnamed.png?t=1780688991)
Caption: 

# Friday charts: the Knickerbocker economy

In 1999, a nosebleed seat to see the Knicks play the Spurs in the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden [reportedly cost](https://x.com/NJHoopsHaven/status/2059099965218075021?s=20) about $495 on the secondary market ([face value](https://www.ebay.com/itm/235085350571) was just $60).

In 2026, a similar seat to see the Knicks play the Spurs in the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden [costs about $8,000](https://seatgeek.com/new-york-knicks-tickets/6-10-2026-new-york-new-york-madison-square-garden/nba/18068096?irclickid=xc7XdR3xaxyZWL4RoJ3dEV04UkuWsqXNu3ULUs0&utm_source=impact&utm_medium=affiliate&utm_campaign=New+York+Post+Network&utm_term=2229206&utm_content=Other&pid=New+York+Post+Network&aid=16137&adid=2229206&irgwc=1&afsrc=1&quantity=2) — a _1,516%_ increase.

Few things have kept pace. 

The consumer price index is up just 94% in that time. The median price of a US house, 153%. Tuition at a private college is about 193% more expensive. The S&P 500 index is up 435%. Even the Nasdaq 100 is up “just” 1,042%.

If consumers are having an affordability crisis, Knicks fans are living through an affordability apocalypse.

Why have sports tickets in general and Knicks tickets in particular gotten so out of reach?

Part of the answer is a simple general-equilibrium effect: The price of a television set to watch games on has fallen by 95% since 1999 (in real terms), which leaves us with more money to spend on going to games. 

But Madison Square Garden still seats just 19,500 Knicks fans, so more money is chasing the same number of tickets.

The advent of smartphones has pushed tickets to the absolute limit of what people are willing to pay. In 1999, price discovery happened between fans and scalpers on the 7th Avenue sidewalk outside MSG. Today, it happens between everyone on SeatGeek (which is everyone).

There are reasons specific to the New York Knicks, as well: Fortuitously positioned between Wall Street and Broadway, the fan base is populated with high-spending investment bankers, corporate lawyers, movie stars and newsletter writers.

There’s also decades of pent-up demand. The Knickerbockers haven’t been in the NBA Finals since 1999. Even more tragically, they haven’t won a title since 1973.

But the biggest factor of all is t
